page: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Daldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 1890 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded decompression and PassMark CPU scores fall into the top 25% tier. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth sit in the middle 50% average tier. This highlights a tradeoff where single-threaded execution is highly optimized, while highly parallelized memory-bound tasks perform at average levels.

Given the 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is positioned as a compute-optimized option with a low-memory profile. It is suited for workloads requiring high clock speeds and local storage, such as static web serving, lightweight database operations, and small-to-medium language model inference, where text generation speeds for 135M and 7B models reach top 10% and top 25% tiers respectively. The inclusion of 1890 GB of local storage provides cost efficiency for data-intensive tasks that do not require massive memory footprints.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 32 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of memory, and 1890 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression benchmarks ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, reflecting a compute-heavy, low-memory design. With a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, it is suited for compute-bound workloads, static web serving, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is a 32-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is a 32-vCPU virtual machine with an AMD EPYC processor, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage for compute-heavy tasks.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency AMD processors, local storage, and strong single-threaded performance for resource-efficient workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Optimized for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads and static web serving
Supports Premium Storage and runs on Microsoft Hyper-V
